steps.title: Atmosphere Primed
narrative: Warm, moist air is surging northward across the Plains.
Dew points in the 60s and 70s are creating an unstable environment.
Strong winds are already present just above the surface.

steps.title: SPC Upgrade
narrative: The Storm Prediction Center has issued a Moderate Risk for severe weather.
Conditions are favorable for widespread damaging winds.
A long-lived, organized storm system is becoming more likely.

steps.title: Storms Begin to Develop
narrative: Early storms are beginning to form along a boundary to the west.
Initial development is scattered, but the environment supports rapid growth.
Watch closely as storms begin organizing.

steps.title: Rapid Intensification
narrative: Storms are quickly intensifying and beginning to merge.
A line is forming, which is a key signal for damaging wind potential.
You can now control time to explore storm evolution.

steps.title: Warnings Issued
narrative: Severe thunderstorm warnings are being issued as storms strengthen.
Damaging winds in excess of 70 mph are possible.
Warnings help alert communities to take action.

steps.title: Live Doppler View
narrative: This is a simulated live Doppler radar sweep.
Notice how the radar scans the storm structure in real time.
This helps meteorologists identify areas of strongest winds.

steps.title: Damage Reports Increase
narrative: Storm reports are now coming in from across the region.
Widespread wind damage is being observed.
These reports confirm the strength of the system.

steps.title: Aftermath
narrative: The storm system continues east, leaving damage behind.
This type of event is known as a derecho.
Understanding these systems helps improve future warnings.
